payload.title: Minutes of the Regular Meeting Joint Long Island Rail Road and Metro-North Committees Monday, December 19, 2022

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.description: One of two railroads reporting to the Joint LIRR/Metro-North Committee
One of two railroads reporting to the Joint LIRR/Metro-North Committee
MTA division responsible for construction and development projects
MTA police department providing law enforcement services

payload.title: Interim President of LIRR and President of Metro-North
payload.description: LIRR Interim President and Metro-North President
Contractor providing FRA-mandated ultrasonic rail testing and joint bar detection services for LIRR and Metro-North
Responsible for approximately 33% of Metro-North's Sperry Rail contract costs

New 700,000 square foot LIRR terminal below Grand Central Terminal. C&D turned over the new terminal to LIRR on December 9, 2022. Inaugural shuttle service between GCM and Jamaica imminent pending completion of systems testing.

Construction of four new ADA compliant Metro-North stations on Amtrak's Hell Gate Line. Upon completion, serves as an extension of the New Haven Line from New Rochelle to Penn Station. Approximately 500,000 people live within one mile of the four new stations in Hunts Point, Parkchester/Van Nest, Morris Park, and Co-Op City.

warnings: project.routes_served is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Route lists should become project-route relations when supported. Suggested relation: serves_route.

payload.description: Completion of the third and final segment of the long awaited third track between Floral Park and Hicksville. 9.8 mile span giving LIRR more operational flexibility, improving safety, minimizing disruptions, and increasing systemwide service by 41% once full GCM service goes live. Creates two way and reverse peak service along the LIRR Main Line.

Six month renewal project to replace the 110 year old Wickers Creek Culvert in Dobbs Ferry, destroyed by Tropical Storm Ida. C&D rebuilt the entire 4-track right of way above the culvert, including reinstalling rails, ties, ballast, third rail and ensuring signals and PTC systems operated flawlessly. Project completion meant faster travel times and increased capacity on the Hudson Line.

tool_args.label: Harlem Line Station Improvements - Hartsdale, Scarsdale, Purdy's

payload.project_name: Harlem Line Station Improvements - Hartsdale, Scarsdale, Purdy's
payload.description: Full slate of improvements to three Harlem Line stations: Hartsdale, Scarsdale and Purdy's. Upgrades include street and platform elevators, ADA compliant sidewalks and curb ramps, new staircases and closed circuit TV as well as increased electrical capacity. Hartsdale and Scarsdale scheduled for substantial completion in Q2 2023, Purdy's in Q3 2023.

Reopening of Breakneck Ridge station following safety enhancement work. Weekend-only stop along the Hudson Line. Station improvements are part of the comprehensive Breakneck Connector segment of the planned 7.5 mile Hudson Highlands Fjord Trail linear park.

tool_args.label: LIRR Concourse at Penn Station Renovation

payload.project_name: LIRR Concourse at Penn Station Renovation
payload.description: Renovation of the LIRR Concourse at Penn Station. Crews widened the concourse to 57 feet, nearly doubling the amount of walking space, and raised the now illuminated ceilings to 18 feet. On time and on budget reopening.

First new LIRR station in almost 50 years. Both east and westbound trains could stop at Elmont-UBS. Will become a regular Hempstead Branch stop 365 days of the year upon full GCM schedule implementation. Enabled by the 15-month Bellerose Ladder switch installation project.

GCM readiness project. Segment of track beneath the new Colonial Road bridge was extended 1,100 feet to accommodate an additional 12 car train. Helps meet the robust service strategy for the Port Washington Branch when GCM service goes live.

tool_args.label: Grand Central Madison Turnover to LIRR

payload.description: MTA C&D turned over the new Grand Central Madison terminal to LIRR, with major work complete save for systems testing and some punch list items.
Ground broken on the new Penn Station Access project, involving construction of four new ADA compliant Metro-North stations on Amtrak's Hell Gate Line.
Celebration surrounding the completion of the third and final segment of the long awaited third track between Floral Park and Hicksville.
LIRR announced that both east and westbound trains could now stop at Elmont-UBS, the first new LIRR station in almost 50 years.
Metro-North held its first Connect With Us customer outreach program since the 2021 onset of Omicron at Stamford.
Inaugural Connect With Us event for LIRR at Hicksville.
MTA Chairman Janno Lieber and President Rinaldi joined Governor Kathy Hochul as she announced the new LIRR terminal below GCT will be known as Grand Central Madison.

Brand new TrainTime app introduced to very positive customer reviews. For the first time, Metro-North and LIRR customers can plan trips, track train movement and car capacity, and buy tickets all in a single app.

tool_args.label: LIRR Concourse at Penn Station Reopening

payload.description: LIRR unveiled a dramatically more modern and spacious LIRR Concourse at Penn Station. Wider to 57 feet, ceilings raised to 18 feet.
Metro-North rebranded its customer call ahead assistance program as Metro-North Care to align it with the existing LIRR Care service.
Both railroads rolled out brand new fare programs featuring 10% off monthly tickets, 20% off a new 20 trip peak ticket, and a newly expanded CityTicket valid during all off peak hours.
LIRR total November on-time performance

payload.claim_text: The combo ticket, once LIRR goes live with full GCM service, will make it even easier for Long Islanders to check out Yankee games or go hiking in the Hudson Valley, and for Metro-North customers to visit Long Island or catch events at UBS Arena. Customers can buy an off peak ticket on the first railroad, with the connecting service on the other railroad being an eight dollar flat fare. This is a pilot that will go for a couple of months.
payload.description: New combo ticket pilot program for cross-railroad travel between LIRR and Metro-North

payload.claim_text: Both railroads rolled out brand new fare programs featuring wallet friendly discounts such as 10% off monthly tickets, 20% off a new 20 trip peak ticket, and a newly expanded CityTicket now valid during all off peak hours and not just on weekends.
payload.description: New fare programs introduced to bring COVID-weary customers back

payload.claim_text: For the first time, Metro-North and LIRR customers are now able to plan their trips, track train movement and car capacity, and buy tickets all in a single app. The new TrainTime app replaces the former MTA eTix and individual trip planning apps for each railroad.
payload.description: TrainTime app replaces MTA eTix with unified trip planning

payload.claim_text: Penn Station Access will serve as an extension of the New Haven Line from New Rochelle to Penn Station. Approximately 500,000 people live within one mile of the four new stations in Hunts Point, Parkchester/Van Nest, Morris Park, and Co-Op City.
payload.description: Description of Penn Station Access project and population served

payload.claim_text: The third track gives LIRR more operational flexibility, improving safety, minimizing disruptions, and increasing systemwide service by 41% once the full GCM service goes live. This addition also creates two way and reverse peak service along the LIRR Main Line, which is a true boon to regional economic growth efforts.
payload.description: Benefits of the LIRR Main Line Third Track

payload.claim_text: The project completion meant faster travel times and increased capacity on the Hudson Line, as Metro-North had to implement a reduced schedule in order to accommodate the six months of repair work.
payload.description: Wickers Creek Culvert replacement restored service and improved travel times

tool_args.label: Metro-North Care Alignment with LIRR Care

payload.claim_text: Metro-North rebranded its customer call ahead assistance program as Metro-North Care to align it with the existing LIRR Care service. This ensures customers with mobility impairments have the same resources and assistance available to them whether they are on the Hudson Line or the Hempstead Branch.
payload.description: Alignment of customer assistance programs between railroads

payload.claim_text: MTAPD hosted Crime Prevention Through Environmental Design training with Metro-North and LIRR partners. Nine teams looked at Tarrytown station, Ronkonkoma station, Grand Central, and Penn Station and made fantastic recommendations to make the stations and spaces safer.
payload.description: MTAPD conducted CPTED training to improve station safety

Committee recommended approval of one year contract extension to joint Metro-North/LIRR contract with Sperry Rail, Inc. in the not to exceed amount of $7,000,000, split $4,000,000 for Metro-North and $3,000,000 for LIRR. CDOT responsible for approximately 33% ($1.3 million) of Metro-North's amount.
